title: High-level pipeline overview for Parameter Golf lane_3 showing: (1) float-only t
caption: High-level pipeline overview for Parameter Golf lane_3 showing: (1) float-only training producing Baseline I; (2) staged QAT with fake quantization starting at training_frac=0.5 leading to Baseline E (QAT+teacher); (3) codec-guided distillation variant F adding an explicit zlib-based compression loss L_comp on selected tensors; (4) optional compression-biased gating variant H with training-time stochastic gates driven by per-channel zlib statistics. The diagram should also depict the int8+zlib deployment roundtrip, the 16MB artifact cap, and the quick-gate runtime constraint (runtime <= 1.10x baseline).
label: fig:pipeline_overview_1

title: Conceptual architecture/method diagram zooming into the codec-guided distillatio
caption: Conceptual architecture/method diagram zooming into the codec-guided distillation (Hypothesis 1). Show a float teacher network and an int8-student undergoing staged QAT. Indicate: (a) the standard distillation path with KL loss between teacher and quantized-student logits; (b) the selection of specific weight tensors or activations from the quantized student, conversion to bytes, zlib.compress call, and computation of bits-per-byte; (c) the teacher- or baseline-relative target alpha and the resulting L_comp = (L_zlib - alpha)^2 term; (d) combination into L_total = L_task + lambda_kd * L_KD + lambda_comp * L_comp. Make it clear that zlib is used sparsely during training and that deployment uses int8+zlib without extra components.
label: fig:architecture_diagram_2

title: Flowchart detailing the compression-biased gate dropout mechanism (Hypothesis 2)
caption: Flowchart detailing the compression-biased gate dropout mechanism (Hypothesis 2). Steps: (1) During selected minibatches, quantize per-channel activations, run zlib on each channel slice to obtain bpb_c, and update EMA statistics h_c; (2) compute biased drop probabilities p_drop(c) = clip(beta * (h_c - mean_h), 0, p_max); (3) sample binary gate g_c ~ Bernoulli(1 - p_drop(c)) and apply to activations during QAT; (4) emphasize that at inference, all g_c are fixed to 1 (no gating), so the deployed graph is unchanged. Include annotations about how this ties into the overall QAT stage and preserves the gate/runtime budget.
label: fig:method_flowchart_3

title: Runtime Overhead and Quick-Gate Constraint
caption: Runtime comparison between the baseline float-only model and the QAT + roundtrip-aligned candidate, plotted in milliseconds with annotations for the runtime ratio and whether the quick-gate constraint (runtime ≤ 1.10× baseline) is satisfied.
label: fig:fig_runtime_quick_gate

title: Validation bpb Comparison: Float Training vs QAT + Roundtrip Alignment
caption: Comparison of validation bits-per-byte (val_bpb) between the baseline float-only model and the QAT + roundtrip-aware compression alignment configuration, both under the same gate budget and artifact size limit. Lower val_bpb indicates better compression performance.
label: fig:fig_main_results_val_bpb

title: Change in Validation bpb Relative to Baseline
caption: Absolute change in validation bits-per-byte (delta_bpb) of the QAT + roundtrip-aligned configuration relative to the float-only baseline, highlighting the net compression benefit after the int8+zlib roundtrip.
label: fig:fig_delta_bpb_vs_baseline

title: Feasibility Under Runtime and Artifact Constraints
caption: Summary of whether the QAT + roundtrip-aligned configuration satisfies the quick-gate runtime constraint and the 16 MB artifact size limit, providing an at-a-glance feasibility check for deployment relative to the baseline.
label: fig:fig_gate_feasibility_overview
